findings.description: When legacy plans lack phases, learn:execute computes phases in memory but does not write them back. Next run repeats upgrade and other tools still see legacy plan structure.
findings.title: Phase assignment upgrade in learn:execute is not persisted
findings.suggested_issue: Persist upgraded phases to plan.json via CLI endpoint (lock-protected + validated).

findings.description: If a process crashes while holding .workflow/learn/.lock, subsequent commands fail with LOCKED after 2s and do not attempt stale-lock recovery.
findings.title: Stale lock file can permanently block learn workflow
findings.suggested_issue: Implement stale lock detection (pid not alive / created_at too old) and provide a safe unlock path.

findings.description: learn:plan uses mv via Bash() to promote the validated plan. This is not portable on Windows and can leave plan.tmp.json behind.
findings.title: Plan promotion uses mv via Bash() (non-portable)
findings.suggested_issue: Replace with Write() to final path or Node rename, and verify success before continuing.

findings.description: learn:profile select uses ls -1 .workflow/learn/profiles/*.json. This is shell-dependent; CLI provides learn:list-profiles.
findings.title: Profile selection enumerates profiles via ls glob instead of CLI list-profiles
findings.suggested_issue: Use ccw learn:list-profiles --json to enumerate profiles.
